paper_title: Inferred Title from Material and Methods and Phylogenetic Analysis Sections

methods.description: Process for examining and preparing a fossil specimen for detailed morphological study using microscopy and casting techniques.

steps.description: The single specimen in part and counterpart was collected during a road construction near Patoenoeang Asoe E at the beginning of the 20th century.
steps.searchable_summary: collect specimen from road construction site

steps.description: The specimen, which is housed in the collections of the Naturalis Biodiversity Center Leiden, The Netherlands, and labelled with the repository number RGM 624420, was examined using a stereomicroscope equipped with camera lucida drawing arm.
steps.searchable_summary: examine specimen using stereomicroscope with camera lucida

steps.description: Casts of the embedded teeth and dermal denticles were prepared using silicon compound and epoxy resin.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare casts of teeth and denticles using silicon and epoxy

steps.description: Studied and photographed with a Hitachi S-3500N Scanning Electronic Microscope (SEM) at the University of Bristol.
steps.searchable_summary: study and photograph specimen using SEM

steps.description: Measurements were taken to the nearest 0.1 mm.

methods.description: Analysis of morphological characters to determine phylogenetic relationships among taxa.

steps.description: The analysis of 102 morphological characters coded for 29 taxa produced only two most parsimonious trees with the same length of 214 steps and with the same relatively high consistency index (CI = 0.65) and retention index (RI = 0.79).
steps.searchable_summary: perform parsimony analysis on morphological data

steps.description: A complete list of synapomorphies for each node is listed.
